summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the tarball root containing GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) inside the targets/sources fields (line 6). npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present, even with no declared install/postinstall script, and node-gyp/GYP evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during its configure step. This causes the embedded command to execute on every `npm install` of the package, with no opt-in from the installer. The package does not appear to ship native C/C++ source that would justify a real node-gyp build, so the binding.gyp's only function is to trigger command execution under the install lifecycle. This is functionally equivalent to a malicious postinstall hook and grants the publisher arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
